mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects robust supply-side momentum that outpaces rate hike concerns, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months do not precede negative returns, or market pricing of rate hikes remains flat despite CPI acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy that forces monetary policy normalization, leading to negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: GDP-unemployment divergence periods show positive forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day returns, or the signal loses predictive power after 2010.
